title: Ordering at a Restaurant
conversation: Tanya: I hope the waiter comes soon. I'm starving! John: Me too! Tanya: Why don't we flag him down? John: Waiter, waiter! Kevin: I'll be right with you! . . . Sorry for the hold up. We're packed to the rafters tonight. John: We noticed! Kevin: I'm Kevin, and I'll be your server tonight. Can I start you off with some drinks? John: I'd like the Sam Adams Summer Ale. Tanya: Make it two. Kevin: And did you decide on some appetizers? John: We're going to skip the appetizers tonight. What do you recommend for a main course? Kevin: We're known for our seafood. The salmon with black bean sauce is our most popular dish. Tanya: Okay, I'll try that. Kevin: Excellent choice. And for you, sir? John: I'm having trouble deciding. I'm in the mood for steak, but I know you pride yourselves on your seafood. Kevin: How about trying the surf and turf? That way, you get the best of both worlds! John: Okay, let's go with that. Kevin: I'll be right back with your drinks. They're on the house because you had to wait so long to order.

idioms.example: I haven't eaten all day, so I'm starving.
idioms.context: I hope the waiter comes soon. I'm starving!

idioms.meaning_en: to signal to someone to stop
idioms.meaning_vi: vẫy tay ra hiệu cho ai đó dừng lại
idioms.example: We need to flag down a taxi if we want to get to the airport on time.
idioms.context: Why don't we flag him down?

idioms.meaning_en: I will help or serve you in a moment
idioms.meaning_vi: Tôi sẽ phục vụ/hỗ trợ bạn ngay đây
idioms.example: Finish your phone call, and I'll be right with you to help with those bags.
There was a major hold up on the highway due to an accident.

idioms.meaning_vi: chật kín người, không còn chỗ trống
idioms.example: The stadium was packed to the rafters for the final game of the season.
idioms.context: We're packed to the rafters tonight.

idioms.idiom: Can I start you off with
idioms.meaning_en: a phrase used by servers to ask for an initial order (usually drinks or appetizers)
idioms.meaning_vi: Tôi có thể bắt đầu phục vụ bạn món... không?
idioms.example: Can I start you off with some fresh bread and water?
idioms.context: Can I start you off with some drinks?

idioms.meaning_en: I would like the same thing as the person who just ordered
idioms.meaning_vi: Cho tôi hai phần giống vậy (hoặc cho tôi phần giống người vừa gọi)
idioms.example: If he's having the chocolate cake, make it two!

idioms.meaning_en: to choose something after thinking about several possibilities
idioms.example: It took a while, but we finally decided on the blue paint for the kitchen.
idioms.context: And did you decide on some appetizers?

idioms.meaning_en: to not do or have something that you usually do or that you should do
idioms.example: I think I'll skip dessert today; I'm too full.
idioms.context: We're going to skip the appetizers tonight.

idioms.meaning_en: famous or recognized for a specific quality or achievement
idioms.example: This region is known for its beautiful vineyards.

idioms.meaning_en: feeling like doing or having something specific
idioms.meaning_vi: đang muốn/có hứng thú với cái gì
idioms.example: Are you in the mood for Italian food tonight?
idioms.context: I'm in the mood for steak, but I know you pride yourselves on your seafood.

idioms.meaning_en: to be proud of a particular quality or skill that you have
idioms.meaning_vi: tự hào về điều gì đó của bản thân/cửa hàng mình
idioms.example: They pride themselves on their excellent customer service.
idioms.context: I know you pride yourselves on your seafood.

idioms.meaning_en: a main course that combines seafood (surf) and meat, usually beef (turf)
idioms.meaning_vi: món ăn kết hợp hải sản và thịt đỏ
idioms.example: I couldn't decide between lobster and steak, so I ordered the surf and turf.
idioms.context: How about trying the surf and turf?

idioms.meaning_en: a situation where you can enjoy the advantages of two very different things at the same time
idioms.meaning_vi: vẹn cả đôi đường, tốt cả hai mặt
idioms.example: Living in the suburbs gives her the best of both worlds: peace and quiet, but close to the city.
idioms.context: That way, you get the best of both worlds!

idioms.meaning_en: let's choose that option or make that decision
idioms.meaning_vi: hãy chọn cái đó, chốt phương án đó
idioms.example: The red dress looks better on you, let's go with that.

idioms.meaning_en: given for free by a business (like a restaurant or bar)
idioms.example: The manager said the drinks are on the house since it's our anniversary.
idioms.context: They're on the house because you had to wait so long to order.
